programmes.show_description: In Oxfordshire, Guy hopes to bring a £6.5m 14-bedroomed, five-acre mega manor house onto his books. And can new broker Kenrick secure the listing of an uber-modern apartment?
Trobiano's: Gordon Ramsay meets struggling executive chef Anthony Trobiano, who's sinking under half a million dollars of debt at his restaurant in Great Neck, New York.
Epic rags-to-riches drama with Brenda Blethyn and Jessica Reynolds. With Britain at war, Emma takes a huge gamble to stay afloat. But it could cost her far more than her business. (Ep7/8)
Epic rags-to-riches drama with Brenda Blethyn and Jessica Reynolds. As the war rages on, Emma battles to keep her empire afloat, but would she really get into bed with the Fairleys? (Ep8/8)
A steam engine enthusiast makes an ambitious journey across Devon in an 80-year-old steamroller. Near Falmouth, two champion free divers explore a spectacular underwater cave.

programmes.show_name: Find It, Fix It, Flog It
programmes.show_description: In Newmarket, Henry Cole and Simon O'Brien uncover unexpected items in Margaret's shed, to raise some serious cash for the local foodbank.
Can Warren trust Jenson? Diane makes a decision. Cleo struggles to re-adjust to life at home. And Donny proposes to Misbah, but it's not long before their bubble bursts...
Colin Murray, Rachel Riley and Susie Dent host, with Levi Roots in Dictionary Corner, as contestants race against the clock to pit their wits against vowels, consonants and numbers.
Personal Business: Angry at the implication that she couldn't get a job anywhere else, Diane quits her post at Cheers and attempts to prove her value in the job market.
Homicidal Ham: Andy, the former prisoner who Sam arranged as a blind date for Diane, returns to the bar and recruits Diane to help him develop a career as an actor.
Sumner's Return: A year after abandoning Diane for his ex-wife, Professor Sumner Sloan returns to Cheers to make amends. But he may have more on his mind than friendship.
Standard Deviation: Robert asks Ray and Debra to complete IQ tests as part of his criminal psychology class, but then tells each of them that they scored the highest mark.
Look, Don't Touch: Debra's suspicious when Angelina, a waiter at Nemo's, calls Ray at home. Although it's all perfectly innocent, Ray feels guilty.
Frank, the Writer: Frank submits ideas for a column to Ray's editor after getting published in Reader's Digest. But Ray's editor thinks it's the worst writing he's ever read.
Sliding Frasiers: In homage to the romantic film Sliding Doors, two radically different versions of Frasier's love life unfold.
Hungry Heart: In a comedy of mistaken identity, Frasier intervenes to save station manager Kenny's marriage by diverting him from a rendezvous.
Hooping Cranes: Frasier takes his dad and Niles to the basketball but can't believe his eyes when Niles is plucked out of the crowd and given the chance to win a big cash prize.
Docu.Drama: Frasier can't narrate Roz's ambitious space documentary, so she turns to legendary space hero and US senator John Glenn.
Ms Jean's Southern Cuisine: Gordon's in Pennsylvania to help rescue a soul food restaurant that was opened 12 years ago by a retired special education teacher.
Channel 4's midday news summary.

programmes.show_name: Find It, Fix It, Flog It
programmes.show_description: In the East Midlands, Henry Cole finds his dream Russian motorbike and sidecar from World War II, while Simon O'Brien snaps up a trendy 1970s fire surround.

programmes.show_name: Matt Baker: Our Farm in the Dales
programmes.show_description: Matt creates the ultimate covered feeding station and the whole family transform old bits and pieces into an adventure playground for their pygmy goat herd.
Colin Murray, Rachel Riley and Susie Dent host, with Levi Roots in Dictionary Corner, as contestants race against the clock to pit their wits against vowels, consonants and numbers.
Loretta and Carl want the perfect Spanish bolthole in Mojácar, where they can relax and unwind whenever they like. Laura Hamilton shows them what their £100k can buy.

programmes.show_name: A New Life in the Sun
programmes.show_description: Can Paul and Fiona meet their deadline for finishing renovations in France? In Portugal, Leah and Joao try to break into the busy Albufeira bar scene.
The new series continues. Donna and Paul race to finish a risky new floor. Terry and Ashley gamble on their most lavish suite yet. Can Sharon and Tim complete their tower suite?
...the Sun: As work progresses, the endless dust, noise and stress take their toll. And after Jon has another setback, the family begin to wonder if the build is really worth it... (Ep7)
Channel 4's flagship news programme. Including the weather.
Keith Brymer Jones and Marj Hogarth crack on with the next stage of their 163-year-old chapel renovation, and throw a gigantic vintage clothes sale to raise much-needed funds.
Ordering a factory-built house was a no-brainer for manufacturing maestro Pete. But, in true self-building style, even a near-finished project brings challenges like a bat out of hell.

programmes.show_name: New: The Hunt: Prey vs Predator
programmes.show_description: Just one hunt stands between the final four players and a prize of up to £100,000. But the last hunt brings a fiendish twist, big-money challenges - and the ultimate test of trust... (Ep9)
The Goggleboxers watch Eurovision, Faking It, Virgin Island, The Four Seasons, Night Coppers, Inside Our Minds, and Naked and Afraid.
This time it's Race Across the World, Married at First Sight Australia, The Apprentice, Last One Laughing, Secrets of the Bees, Just One Thing and Celebrity Ex on the Beach.
Gamer Lauren is looking for her perfect player number two to help her reboot her love life. And Ryan is searching for a silver fox with a dad bod.
...Experiment: Chelsea and George seek a threesome, but will it help Chelsea move past George's sexually adventurous past and infidelity? Jess and Ben host a naked dinner party.
Josh Pugh, Lorraine Kelly and Steve Bracknall join Adam Hills, Josh Widdicombe and Alex Brooker for a tongue-in-cheek look at the week's news.
In South Wales, there's a power tussle in the kitchen, a shared appreciation of bargains, and a few home truths, as three couples compete to host the best dinner party.

programmes.show_name: Kirstie's Fill Your House for Free
programmes.show_description: Kirstie Allsopp and the team come to the rescue of Dave and Kate, whose house has been flooded.

programmes.show_name: George Clarke's Old House New Home
programmes.show_description: George meets builder Anthony and physiotherapist Emily, who are determined to turn their ramshackle 17th-century cottage in Warwickshire into their family home.
Colin Murray, Rachel Riley and Susie Dent host, with Levi Roots in Dictionary Corner, as contestants race against the clock to pit their wits against vowels, consonants and numbers.
Affairs of the Heart: When a classy customer falls for Carla, no one can work out why she's not interested. But it seems she's convinced that he must have some terrible flaw.
Old Flames: Sam's old buddy Dave insists that he can break up Sam's romance with Diane in just 24 hours, especially when Diane discovers Sam still has his 'little black book'.
Manager Coach: When Coach gets the chance to manage a youth league baseball team, his competitive nature soon turns him from a loveable guy into an angry tyrant.
Your Place or Mine?: When Ray's parents fall out, his mum moves in with him and Debra. But Debra isn't happy and tries to convince Ray's dad to apologise.
In-Laws: Debra's well-to-do parents come to visit. Ray begs his own parents to be nice to them, but when they all go out for a meal, it proves a tall order...
Win, Lose or Draw: Ray decides to ignore his dad's poker advice in order to prove he doesn't need any help. But when he loses yet again, Debra wants him to ask for his money back.
It Takes Two to Tangle: Frasier and Niles desperately search for a wealthy patron to lend support to the failing prep school they both attended.
Forgotten But Not Gone: Frasier resigns from his beloved wine club to avoid a conflict of interest when he begins a new radio show about wine.
Daphne Returns: Niles's romantic chances could be scuppered when Daphne's return sparks a squabble that could end it all.
The Wizard and Roz: Frasier seeks advice from his mentor, who seems more interested in Roz. Daphne tries to convince Niles that she's psychic.
Barefoot Bob's: Gordon visits Barefoot Bob's beachside restaurant in Massachusetts, where husband and wife Marc and Lisa struggle to attract customers during the winter months.
Channel 4's midday news summary.

programmes.show_name: Find It, Fix It, Flog It
programmes.show_description: In Leyland, Simon O'Brien transforms three battered car seats into a three-piece suite. And Henry Cole repurposes an overhead light into a fashionable light box.

programmes.show_name: Matt Baker: Our Farm in the Dales
programmes.show_description: The Bakers call in some local storm-proofing experts to safeguard the farmhouse and Matt takes his dad to see the progress that's been made on his 1946 Dodge.
Colin Murray, Rachel Riley and Susie Dent host, with Levi Roots in Dictionary Corner, as contestants race against the clock to pit their wits against vowels, consonants and numbers.
Anne-Marie and Adrian consider Los Cristianos in southern Tenerife their second home... so why not buy one there? Can Jean Johansson find the perfect place for £160k?

programmes.show_name: A New Life in the Sun
programmes.show_description: In Spain, demolitions get underway at Tom and Terry's B&B. And eight years of building works finish in France, but can Paul and Fiona successfully transition into hosting?
The new series continues. Terry and Ashley finish their suite and rehang a 200-crystal chandelier. Belinda and Lee tackle tower mould. Can Vivienne and Simon complete their mural?
...the Sun: With Jon back to health, things finally start to look up. Work starts in the garden and Jasmine's carpenter brother Andrew helps out making bookshelves for Joy's bedroom. (Ep8)
Channel 4's flagship news programme. Including the weather.

programmes.show_name: New: Help! I Bought It at...
programmes.show_description: ...Auction with Sarah Beeny: In the South Downs National Park, Michelle and Derek take on a cosy cottage in need of some TLC. Archie and Lauren try to add value to their fixer-upper. (Ep3)
Claymore: Spy thriller with Simu Liu. When top-secret intelligence agency The Orphanage becomes convinced that it has a mole, analyst Alexander Hale realises he's the prime suspect. (Ep1/8)
Glass House: Spy thriller. Alexander and The Orphanage are forced to work together to protect their discovery, setting a deadly trap with Alexander and Michelle as bait. (Ep2/8)
